pretty big update lol\
- Fixed clip trims being rounded or snapped incorrectly, including cases where a `7.34s` trim was being overwritten as `7.00s`
- Fixed inability to crop clips under 7 seconds
- Fixed delayed audio in edited/exported clips by changing trim/export handling
- Fixed left-to-right and right-to-left trim handle math so handles no longer snap to `0.00s` or drag from the wrong side
- Fixed timeline state so trim/playhead positions snap back into valid bounds after dragging
- Fixed playback so only the currently trimmed portion is playable in the editor
- Fixed playback so if the start of a clip is trimmed out, pressing play starts from the crop start instead of the original file start
- Fixed single-segment timeline behavior after split/delete so the remaining segment snaps correctly to the beginning when appropriate
- Fixed deleting the final segment so stale preview frames are cleared instead of lingering in the viewport
- Fixed a preview bug where some clips would not play until another clip was selected and reselected
- Fixed processing clips so they cannot be played while save/overwrite is still running
- Fixed overwrite behavior so it stages a new file first, then replaces the old file after the new one is ready
- Fixed overwrite flow so the selected clip reloads correctly after overwrite completes
- Added retry/backoff for final overwrite replacement when files are temporarily locked
- Added timeout protection for hung `ffmpeg` edit/export jobs so they do not hold files forever
- Fixed overwrite backup artifacts from appearing as fake clips in the clip library
- Fixed clip library refresh to update in place instead of clearing/rebuilding the whole list, reducing flicker and state churn
- Fixed selecting a processing clip so it no longer wipes the current clipâ€™s undo history
- Fixed background save/overwrite UX so long processing does not leave the user guessing whether anything is happening
- Added processing overlay on the selected clip viewport with spinner and `Processing...`
- Added processing overlay on clip thumbnails in the library
- Kept other clips interactable while a different clip is processing
- Added `Open file location` for the selected clip
- Replaced separate `Save as new` / `Overwrite original` buttons with one `Save` button that opens a small chooser
- Removed `(space)` text from the play button
- Removed the old broken always-visible volume slider
- Added a new hover-only viewport volume control that fades/slides in from the bottom-left with a vignette
- Improved the clip viewport border with a cleaner curved gray frame
- Added startup splash screen with centered logo, dark blue background, and circular loading indicator
- Made startup splash respect a minimum visible duration while still only staying up when real loading is happening
- Added a yellow settings warning shown only while clipping is active, telling users settings cannot be changed during capture
- Added `Start All` and `Stop All` controls for all monitors on the capture screen
- Added structured logging system for debugging and AI-assisted diagnosis
- Added log viewer window in Settings
- Added log export to `.txt`
- Added log session codes in log filenames so logs can be referenced by short code
- Added `Copy Log Path` in the log viewer
- Expanded logs with more save/export/overwrite diagnostics, timing, runtime context, and failure detail
- Made log viewer text white for readability
- Fixed app window startup positioning so it opens centered without the top being cut offscreen
- Tightened the editor timeline layout so timestamps sit closer to the clip strip and controls
- Lowered the package/app target floor to Windows `10.0.19041.0`
- Kept borderless capture support in place while making Store packaging safer around it
